mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond a 0.4% monthly threshold, anticipated monetary tightening compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of unemployment falling below 5.0% and nonfarm payrolls increasing by more than 200,000 in a single month signals robust wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 20-day forward returns in backtests spanning 2010 to 2021.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding above its 12-month moving average indicates strengthening corporate output capacity, which translates into higher quarterly earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO outperformance relative to its 12-month average fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as market participants price in durable goods demand and economic resilience.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a rank correlation coefficient consistently below 0.01 across rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A retail sales surge exceeding 1.5% month-over-month confirms robust consumer demand, leading equity markets to price in higher corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Retail sales spikes above 1.5% month-over-month are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, labor market tightness forces firms to absorb higher wage costs without proportional productivity gains, compressing profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Unemployment rates below 4.0% consistently coincide with positive 20-day forward returns, invalidating the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and real GDP growth signals synchronized supply-side recovery, reducing input cost pressures and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative information coefficient for 5-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows since 2005.
